questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. s<u>u</u>bway
B. l<u>u</u>ggage
C. p<u>u</u>blic
D. s<u>u</u>pport
questions.explanation: The 'u' in support is pronounced differently than in subway, luggage, and public.

questions.question:
Choose the word whose underlined part is pronounced differently:
A. backpack<u>s</u>
B. ticket<u>s</u>
C. custom<u>s</u>
D. passport<u>s</u>
questions.explanation: The 's' in customs is pronounced as /z/.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
 A. around
 B. boarding
 C. baggage
 D. local
questions.explanation: Around is stressed on the second syllable. The others are stressed on the first.

questions.question:
Choose the word that has a different stressed syllable from the rest:
 A. environment
 B. transportation
 C. reliable
 D. convenient
questions.explanation: Transportation has primary stress on the fourth syllable.

questions.question: Make sure you don’t take the wrong bag at the __________.
questions.explanation: We pick up our bags at the baggage claim.

questions.question: Bicycles are __________ for the environment than cars.
questions.explanation: Comparative form of 'good' is 'better'.

questions.question: For commuting in our city, __________ form of transportation is the bus because it’s _________ than going by car or taxi.
questions.explanation: Superlative 'the most popular' and comparative 'cheaper' fit the sentence perfectly.

questions.question: There are __________ buses from Tân Sơn Nhất airport to the city center. Buses depart every 15 minutes.
questions.explanation: Frequent means happening often (every 15 minutes).

questions.question: Korea has excellent __________. The buses are regular and the trains are on time.
questions.explanation: Both buses and trains belong to public transportation.

questions.question:
Person A: Is this Andy’s backpack?
Person B: No, it isn’t. He has a _________ backpack.
questions.explanation: Order of adjectives: Size (big) -> Age (new) -> Color (dark blue).

questions.question:
Person A: Whose suitcase is this?
Person B: It has Jodie and Jack’s names on it, so it must be _________.
questions.explanation: We use the possessive pronoun 'theirs' instead of 'their suitcase'.

questions.question: Officers will check your luggage when you go through __________.
questions.explanation: Customs officers check luggage for forbidden items.

questions.question: It’s very important to keep your __________ in a safe place. You need this document to enter or leave a foreign country.
questions.explanation: A passport is the official travel document to enter or leave countries.

questions.question:
Person A: Excuse me, does this bag belong to you?
Person B: _________. Thank you a lot.
questions.explanation: 'Mine' is the correct possessive pronoun here.

questions.question: What does a sign with crossed-out scissors and knives on a plane mean?
options: You can’t use scissors and knives at the same time on the plane.
You can’t buy any scissors or knives on the plane.
You can’t bring any sharp objects on the plane.
You can’t use scissors or knives because they are sharp.
questions.correctAnswer: You can’t bring any sharp objects on the plane.
questions.explanation: This signifies prohibiting sharp objects on flights for safety.

questions.question: What does a Speed Limit 65, Night 70 sign mean?
options: You have to drive slower at night than during the day.
You can’t drive faster than 70 miles per hour at night.
You have to go at 65 kilometers per hour at night.
The fastest speed you can drive is 65 miles per hour.
questions.correctAnswer: You can’t drive faster than 70 miles per hour at night.
questions.explanation: The sign indicates a nighttime speed limit of 70.

questions.question: I think electric cars are more __________ than petrol cars because they don’t produce air pollution. (FRIEND)
Buses in this country aren’t ___________. They’re often late. (RELY)
Traveling by train is the best option for long-distance travel. They are clean, __________ and punctual. (COMFORT)
MTR, with ten lines, is the most __________ way of traveling in Hongkong. (CONVENIENCE)
I like traveling by motorbike because it’s a great way to get around Hồ Chí Minh city __________. (QUICK)
In the UK, there are several __________ kinds of public transportation. (DIFFER)

questions.transcript: I take my electric scooter for short trips because I like to drive around the city.
questions.question: Private transportation is better for ______ distances.

questions.transcript: However, if traveling long distances, I like public transportation.
questions.question: Public transportation is better for ______ distances.

questions.transcript: I take my electric scooter for short trips
questions.question: Example of private transportation: an electric ______.

questions.transcript: ...faster and more eco-friendly transportation like a train.
questions.question: Public transportation is more ______.

questions.transcript: But if you’re traveling a long way, it's better to take public transportation like a bus or train.
questions.question: Example of public transportation: a bus or a ______.
Listen to the conversation. TRUE or FALSE? The main topic of the conversation is about two guys traveling around the city.
questions.explanation: The conversation is about traveling around the country on buses and trains.

questions.question: Steve had a wonderful time using public transportation to get around.
questions.explanation: Steve expressly states: 'I’ve had a wonderful time using public transportation to travel around.'

questions.question: Locals like to take the train because it’s cheap.
questions.explanation: Locals like to use buses because they are cheap, not trains.

questions.question: Trains are usually not near the cities.
questions.explanation: Steve mentions trains are usually away from cities, so you get a chance to see farms.

questions.question: The food on a train is better than on a bus.
questions.explanation: They do not talk about food on the train or bus.

questions.passage:
Transportation has changed in many ways over the years. The oldest known form of transportation is the boat. Around 60,000 years ago, boats were made by hollowing out a tree trunk, also known as a canoe. Riders had to use paddles to control the canoe across the water. However, today boats are more convenient because they have an engine that powers them.

Next came horses. Surprisingly it took 55,000 years after the invention of the boat to discover horses as a means of transportation. At first, horses were ridden without a saddle, also called riding bareback. But over time, riders used saddles to make traveling more comfortable and easier to control the horse. After that, it didn’t take long for us to figure out we could use a wagon and a horse to help carry equipment and people.
questions.question: This article is about how much ______ has changed over the years.

questions.passage: Last summer, I was planning on taking an airplane for the first time, but there were many things I didn’t know. For example, how many suitcases should I take or what can I bring in my backpack? So, I asked my mom for help, and she told me that the more luggage you bring, the more expensive the ticket is. So, she suggested bringing only the things I needed. She also told me not to take dangerous items like large batteries or sharp objects because I’ll have to go through customs. My mom also said not to lose my boarding pass or passport because I’d need them to get on the plane. Finally, she told me I needed to find the baggage claim after the airplane landed to pick up my suitcases. I’m happy my mom was able to help me know more about air traveling because it made the experience better.
questions.question: The more luggage you bring,...
options: the more expensive the ticket is.
the more trouble you can get in.
questions.correctAnswer: the more expensive the ticket is.
questions.explanation: Mom said the more luggage you bring, the more expensive the ticket is.

questions.question: What is true about the story?
options: It’s important to use the seatbelt.
There are many places to eat at the airport.
There are many things to know about flying on an airplane.
You can get lost at the airport.
questions.correctAnswer: There are many things to know about flying on an airplane.
questions.explanation: The passage outlines various rules and recommendations before flying.

questions.question: Rewrite using FAST: During the rush hours in New York, cycling to work and going by bus take the same amount of time.
correctAnswers: During the rush hours in New York, cycling to work is as fast as going by bus.
cycling to work is as fast as going by bus

questions.question: Rewrite using CHEAPER: An underground train across London costs £6 while a taxi costs £15.
correctAnswers: An underground train across London is cheaper than a taxi.
Traveling across London by underground train is cheaper than traveling by taxi.

questions.question:
Rewrite: Heathrow Airport is currently busier than any other airports in the UK.
Heathrow Airport is currently the...
correctAnswers: Heathrow Airport is currently the busiest airport in the UK.

questions.question:
Rewrite: No one else in my family drives better than my father.
My father is...
correctAnswers: My father is the best driver in my family.
the best driver in my family

questions.question: Make a sentence: I / think / travel / bus / be / relaxing / than / car.
correctAnswers: I think traveling by bus is more relaxing than traveling by car.
I think traveling by bus is more relaxing than by car.

questions.question: Make a sentence: This / be / not / my mother / handbag / because / hers / be / brown.
correctAnswers: This is not my mother's handbag because hers is brown.
This isn't my mother's handbag because hers is brown.

questions.question: Make a sentence: Subway / be / popular / means of transportation / New York.
correctAnswers: Subway is the most popular means of transportation in New York.
Subway is a popular means of transportation in New York.

questions.question: Make a sentence: I / prefer / ride my bike / school / because / it / healthy / take the bus.
correctAnswers: I prefer riding my bike to school because it's healthier than taking the bus.
I prefer riding my bike to school because it is healthier than taking the bus.
